root_text.sn44.4:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto, āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko bārāṇasiyaṁ viharanti isipatane migadāye …pe…
root_text.sn44.4:1.3: “Ko nu kho, āvuso, hetu, ko paccayo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.sn44.4:1.4: “Rūpaṁ kho, āvuso, ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, rūpasamudayaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, rūpanirodhaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, rūpanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti;
root_text.sn44.4:1.6: ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti;
root_text.sn44.4:1.7: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.4:1.11: viññāṇaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, viññāṇasamudayaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, viññāṇanirodhaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, viññāṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ajānato apassato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti;
root_text.sn44.4:1.13: ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti;
root_text.sn44.4:1.14: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.4:2.1: Rūpañca kho, āvuso, jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, rūpasamudayaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, rūpanirodhaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, rūpanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.4:2.2: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti.
root_text.sn44.4:2.6: viññāṇaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, viññāṇasamudayaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, viññāṇanirodhaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, viññāṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ jānato passato yathābhūtaṁ, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti;
root_text.sn44.4:2.7: ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti;
root_text.sn44.4:2.8: ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti;
root_text.sn44.4:2.9: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti.
root_text.sn44.4:2.10: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu ayaṁ paccayo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti.

translation_text.sn44.4:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita were staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana. …
translation_text.sn44.4:1.3: “What’s the cause, reverend, what’s the reason why this has not been declared by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn44.4:1.4: “Reverend, not truly knowing and seeing form, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation, one thinks ‘a realized one still exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:1.5: ‘A realized one no longer exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:1.6: ‘a realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:1.7: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.4:1.8: Not truly knowing or seeing feeling …
translation_text.sn44.4:1.11: consciousness, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation, one thinks ‘a realized one still exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:1.12: ‘A realized one no longer exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:1.13: ‘a realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:1.14: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.4:2.6: consciousness, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation, one doesn’t think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:2.7: ‘A realized one no longer exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:2.8: ‘a realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death’ or
translation_text.sn44.4:2.9: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.4:2.10: This is the cause, this is the reason why this has not been declared by the Buddha.”
